text:
All DeFi products reduce down to two things which have a chicken and egg relationship:

1) Yield

2) Liquidity

If a DeFi product has users, chances are it offers one if not both of those things.

Liquidity is the harder product, but it can’t exist without yield. 

In some cases the yield is a public apy value that anyone can capture (think Aave/Morpho deposits).

In some cases the yield is in the back ground as a spread fuelling market makers who are capturing it.

In even more cases the yield is a subsidy for LPs to produce liquidity that’s the ultimate product being repackaged and offered to users.

Liquidity is DeFi’s most important product.

Because without liquidity, users will pretend you don’t have a product.

From this perspective every DeFi product launched since 2021, every point farm, every airdrop, every GTM will make total sense.

Sometimes it can be useful to think reductively because it helps you zero in on priorities.

We are just 5 days away from @Trueo_’s biggest product release. A feature that I’ve imagined since I first entered the DeFi space is about to be real.

And now I have one priority and one priority only: liquidity.

The final boss, but also the first boss.

“Show me the liquidity, and I’ll show you the users.”

Prediction markets are the hottest meta (no pun intended) next to AI.

The cool thing about that is how synergistic the two verticals are.

AI is so integrated into @Trueo_ that every advancement in AI directly translates to increased capabilities in our app.

RT @0xWives: What makes a good dev: 

Does not launch 100 diff projects 1 after the next after first one fails. 

Is not greedy 

Does not…

Relatively speaking, from a competition stand point the EF cutting its budget in 2026 doesn’t hinder its ability to grow or compete.

Why? 

Because unlike 2021 or 2024, altL1’s are under even more market pressure than Ethereum. 

They can’t simply tap into locked supplies for endless VC investments, and the market is losing confidence in their plans.

On top of that, the disparity in roadmaps calls into question whether they are directly competing at all. 

Solana being compared to Hyperliquid at this point (to the detriment of SOL), while is ETH moving into a class of its own.

Philosophically ETH may not appeal to idealists as much as BTC, but in the near future philosophy will begin to matter less as ETH becomes practically more censorship resistant, more private, and more sustainable economically.
War time V

The combination of leading EVM wallets have by far the most distribution in all of crypto.

More than Bitcoin, more than Binance.

If privacy on Ethereum becomes as simple as firing a transaction in your favorite wallet, what stops ETH from being the most superior privacy coin?

Polymarket is really starting to bite off more than they can chew with some of these event contracts that they’re unable to resolve fairly.

My feed is now flooded with examples of dubious resolutions.

Let me not mince words here: 

UMA is an oracle that has not improved since its release 6 years ago. 

Polymarket’s approach to rule alterations adds insult to injury, making legitimate forecasting impossible. It’s textbook “moving the goal posts,” and in inconsistent directions at that.

Everyone is speculating as to whether Polymarket adopts a new resolution system, but based on their attitude towards current resolutions, any new system they introduce will likely have the same shortcomings.
